title: From Sunset to Star Rise by Christina Rossetti — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
Go from me, summer friends, and tarry not:
I am no summer friend, but wintry cold,
A silly sheep benighted from the fold,
A sluggard with a thorn-chok

title: From “Later Life” by Christina Rossetti — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
VI
We lack, yet cannot fix upon the lack:
Not this, nor that; yet somewhat, certainly.
We see the things we do not yearn to see

title: Spring Quiet by Christina Rossetti — Nature Poetry Lines
content:
Gone were but the Winter,
Come were but the Spring,
I would go to a covert
Where the birds sing;

Where in the whitethorn
Singeth a thrush,
And a robi

title: A Better Ressurection by Christina Rossetti — Sad Poetry Lines
No, Thank You John by Christina Rossetti — Love Poetry Lines
content:
I never said I loved you, John:
Why will you tease me day by day,
And wax a weariness to think upon
With always "do" and "pray"?

You Know I never lov

title: Before The Paling Of The Stars by Christina Rossetti — Spiritual Poetry Lines
content:
Before the winter morn,
Before the earliest cock crow,
Jesus Christ was born:
Born in a stable,
Cradled in a manger,
In the world his hands had made

title: The Thread of Life by Christina Rossetti — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
I
The irresponsive silence of the land,
The irresponsive sounding of the sea,
Speak both one message of one sense to me:--
Aloof, aloof, we stand aloo

title: Winter: My Secret by Christina Rossetti — Deep Poetry Lines
content:
I tell my secret? No indeed, not I:
Perhaps some day, who knows?
But not today; it froze, and blows, and snows,
And you're too curious: fie!
